title: panic when removing remote network
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I'm able to create a client side panic when I try to remove a network using `--remote` when accessing a rootless podman server.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Enable rootless podman socket on the server
2. Create network `foo` on the server
3. Connect from client to server (add connection)
3. Try to delete the network `podman --remote network rm foo`

**Describe the results you received:**
```
panic: runtime error: index out of range [0] with length 0

goroutine 1 [running]:
github.com/containers/podman/v2/pkg/domain/infra/tunnel.(*ContainerEngine).NetworkRm(0xc00012e690, 0x557dd47a1800, 0xc0005e5800, 0xc0002ffe40, 0x1, 0x2, 0xc000322b01, 0xc0005f7c80, 0x557dd3637619, 0xc00052d400, ...)
	pkg/domain/infra/tunnel/network.go:31 +0x28c
github.com/containers/podman/v2/cmd/podman/networks.networkRm(0x557dd5130d00, 0xc0002ffe40, 0x1, 0x2, 0x0, 0x0)
	cmd/podman/networks/rm.go:48 +0xb0
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).execute(0x557dd5130d00, 0xc00003c1c0, 0x2, 0x2, 0x557dd5130d00, 0xc00003c1c0)
	vendor/github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:838 +0x47c
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).ExecuteC(0x557dd5138b00, 0xc0000460f0, 0x557dd4521a60, 0x557dd51e7510)
	vendor/github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:943 +0x336
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).Execute(...)
	vendor/github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:883
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).ExecuteContext(...)
	vendor/github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:876
main.Execute()
	cmd/podman/root.go:86 +0xee
main.main()
	cmd/podman/main.go:77 +0x195
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
The network should be deleted.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
(client) ➜ ~ podman --version
podman version 2.1.1
(client) ➜ ~ ssh server podman --version
podman version 2.1.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**
Server:
```

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(client) ➜ ~ pacman -Q podman
podman 2.1.1-1
(server) ➜ ~ pacman -Q podman
podman 2.1.1-1
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Server and client are two local laptops.

comments.body: @zhangguanzhang Is that already merged into the main branch?
Fixed in Main.  Might be backported to podman 2.1.2
